purpose: fixed 2-hop+ multi-file evaluation set for bounded project-slice PDG work

defaults.note: keep engine differences out of this set; G5-8 handles engine-consistency baselines

cases.focus: three-file wrapper return-flow that needs one bridge-completion file beyond the direct boundary

initial_source.src/core.rs: pub fn core(a: i32) -> i32 { a + 1 }

cases.current_gap: direct-boundary scope can include adapter.rs, but without selecting core.rs as bridge-completion the return-ish flow often falls back to plain call-only explanation

want_after_g5: three-file slice includes core.rs as an explicit completion file
propagation lane bridges main.rs use(x) through adapter.rs and back to main.rs def(out)
witness or edge provenance is not limited to plain call_graph

cases.focus: adapter-mediated imported result continuation into a caller-side alias chain

initial_source.src/value.rs: pub fn make(a: i32) -> i32 { a + 1 }

repro.optional_extension: add `let alias2 = alias;` if a longer caller-side alias chain is needed

cases.current_gap: direct-boundary scope can stop at adapter.rs, leaving the imported result from value.rs weakly stitched into the caller-side alias chain

want_after_g5: result of value::make(x) survives the adapter boundary and continues into y -> alias -> out
witness path shows the adapter-mediated continuation
alias continuation does not add stale or broad def-to-def edges
avoid: call edge only explanation that stops at adapter::wrap
alias edges unrelated to the intended local chain

cases.focus: three-file require_relative chain with a mid-file temp alias and a leaf-file return-ish flow

repro.builder: split analyzer_hard_cases_callees_chain_alias_return.rb so the return-ish expression lives in leaf.rb and the temp alias lives in mid.rb

cases.current_gap: direct-boundary scope can stop at the mid file, leaving the leaf return-ish flow outside the enriched slice even though the callers chain depends on it

want_after_g5: mid-file temp alias and leaf-file return-ish flow appear in one bounded slice
witness or provenance data shows where the data bridge entered the callers chain
require_relative fallback does not replace graph-first selection
avoid: chain collapse around the mid-file temp alias
broad fan-out beyond the intended linear chain

cases.focus: three-file dynamic send/public_send separation guard under a bounded 2-hop slice

repro.builder: split analyzer_hard_cases_dynamic_send_public_send.rb so target definitions live in targets.rb and dispatch calls live in router.rb

cases.current_gap: once a bounded slice reaches targets.rb, the system must preserve target-specific separation instead of smearing all dynamic targets together

want_after_g5: target_sym and target_str remain distinguishable under a three-file slice
local-var mediated target edges stay specific
avoid: all dynamic targets collapsing into one broad impacted set
